title: The One with the Ball

jokes.text: I can't pull off "baby doll", can I?
jokes.setup: Chandler tries out a pet name for his girlfriend
jokes.punchline: He immediately recognizes his failure at romantic language
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler self-awareness. Good character integration showing his awkwardness with romance.

jokes.text: No, I think we learned that from the "sugar lips" incident.
jokes.setup: Chandler's question about whether he can pull off pet names
jokes.punchline: Reference to an even worse previous attempt
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy - 'sugar lips incident' implies a whole cringey backstory in three words.

jokes.text: How you doing, pumpkin?
jokes.setup: The established pattern of Chandler failing at pet names
jokes.punchline: Joey casually succeeds at what Chandler can't do
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast - Joey's natural ease vs Chandler's awkwardness.

jokes.text: Because she cries when they die. Then there's the whole funeral.
jokes.setup: Advice not to get Phoebe flowers
jokes.punchline: The absurd extent of Phoebe's flower grief including funerals
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe characterization through Monica's description. The escalation from crying to funeral is well-crafted.

jokes.text: Gary's gonna ask you to move in with him!
jokes.setup: Monica promised not to tell Phoebe about Gary's plan
jokes.punchline: She immediately blurts it out
jokes.explanation: Classic Monica inability to keep secrets. Predictable but well-executed character moment.

jokes.text: He made me promise not to tell, but I couldn't hold it in any longer!
jokes.setup: Monica just broke her promise
jokes.punchline: She explains why, making it worse by emphasizing how quickly she broke it
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Monica's secret-keeping failure. Character-specific behavior.

jokes.text: Right? Because it's fast. Because it's so fast. It's fast.
jokes.setup: Monica trying to justify why the relationship is problematic
jokes.punchline: She repeats 'fast' three different ways, showing her frantic overthinking
jokes.explanation: Shows Monica's anxiety through repetition. Slightly verbose but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Relax. It's Phoebe, not you.
jokes.setup: Monica freaking out about the relationship speed
jokes.punchline: Joey points out she's treating it like it's her relationship
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy and insight. Joey cutting through Monica's projection with simple observation.

jokes.text: I'll ask you to talk to him!
jokes.setup: Phoebe saying she'll handle the situation herself
jokes.punchline: Her solution is to immediately ask someone else to do it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe logic - claiming independence while immediately delegating. Good misdirection.

jokes.text: Because you are so afraid of commitment. You talk to him. Make him scared like you, make him a man.
jokes.setup: Phoebe explaining why Chandler should talk to Gary
jokes.punchline: The backwards logic that being scared of commitment makes someone manly
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe twisted logic. The phrase 'make him a man' by making him scared is brilliantly backwards.

jokes.text: I'm less afraid of commitment than I used to be. That is so sweet. Still terrified.
jokes.setup: Chandler claiming he's improved with commitment
jokes.punchline: Immediately admitting he's still terrified after the sweet reaction
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection. Chandler's honesty undermining his attempt at growth.

jokes.text: That's why they call it Staten "Island".
jokes.setup: Joey asking if Staten Island is really an island
jokes.punchline: Ross's condescending explanation of the obvious
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross pedantry. Simple but character-appropriate condescension.

jokes.text: Oh, I thought it was like Long Island. Also an island.
jokes.setup: Joey's confusion about Staten Island
jokes.punchline: His example of Long Island also being an actual island, making his confusion worse
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of Joey's geographical confusion. Ross's deadpan correction makes it even funnier.

jokes.text: We haven't dropped this ball for an hour. I realized it a half-hour ago, but I didn't say anything. I didn't wanna jinx it.
jokes.setup: The realization they've been playing with the ball for an hour
jokes.punchline: Chandler's superstitious logic about not wanting to jinx their streak
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the episode's main plot. Chandler's superstition feels authentic.

jokes.text: That's the first time I've ever missed a meal. I think my pants are a little loose.
jokes.setup: They forgot about lunch due to the ball game
jokes.punchline: Joey claiming his pants are already loose from missing one meal
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey food obsession. The immediate physical consequence is absurd but in character.

jokes.text: You bought Shaun Cassidy!
jokes.setup: Rachel saying she bought something she's wanted since childhood
jokes.punchline: Chandler's assumption it's a 70s teen heartthrob
jokes.explanation: Decent misdirection but relies on pop culture reference. Age-specific humor.

jokes.text: What is it? What the hell is that? It's a cat. That is not a cat!
jokes.setup: Rachel reveals her expensive purchase
jokes.punchline: Everyone's horrified reaction to the hairless sphinx cat
jokes.explanation: Strong visual comedy. The escalating denial of its cat-ness is funny.

jokes.text: Why is it inside out?
jokes.setup: Everyone's horror at the hairless cat
jokes.punchline: Joey's innocent description of the cat as 'inside out'
jokes.explanation: 'Inside out' is a brilliantly simple description of a hairless cat. Highly quotable and original visual.

jokes.text: Thousand bucks. On a cat? It's not a cat!
jokes.setup: The reveal of the cat's price
jokes.punchline: Chandler's shock and Rachel's continued denial that it's a cat
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with the price reveal. 'It's not a cat!' becoming a running denial.

jokes.text: Free cats do that too, you know. It's not a cat!
jokes.setup: Rachel describing normal cat behavior
jokes.punchline: Chandler's practical observation followed by Rachel's continued denial
jokes.explanation: Good practical humor from Chandler. The running gag continues effectively.

jokes.text: What am I gonna call her? Fluffy?
jokes.setup: Rachel planning to name the hairless cat
jokes.punchline: The ironic suggestion of 'Fluffy' for a completely hairless animal
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony. 'Fluffy' for a hairless cat is economical and clever.

jokes.text: I have to pee. And Rachel's in the bathroom!
jokes.setup: They're committed to not dropping the ball
jokes.punchline: Joey's physical desperation while having to keep playing
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy setup. Joey's predicament escalates the ball game stakes.

jokes.text: Man, I didn't think we'd make it. I know. Don't switch hands, okay?
jokes.setup: Joey's bathroom emergency
jokes.punchline: The implication that Joey is using the bathroom while still playing ball
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the bathroom emergency. The 'don't switch hands' implies the awkward logistics.

jokes.text: Hey, ladies. What are you in here for?
jokes.setup: Chandler approaching Gary in what appears to be a police station
jokes.punchline: Treating it like they're both criminals in jail
jokes.explanation: Good situational humor. Chandler's jail pickup line in a police station.

jokes.text: Are you crazy? Are you insane? If you live with Phoebe, you two are gonna be living together.
jokes.setup: Chandler trying to talk Gary out of moving in with Phoebe
jokes.punchline: His circular logic that living together means living together
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler nervous rambling. The tautology is absurd but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: You get home, she's there. You go to bed, she's there. You wake up and oh, yes, she's there.
jokes.setup: Chandler explaining the 'horrors' of living together
jokes.punchline: Describing constant companionship as if it's a nightmare
jokes.explanation: Good escalating structure. Chandler's commitment phobia making love sound terrifying.

jokes.text: I know. I can't wait! Were your parents happy or something?
jokes.setup: Gary's enthusiasm about constant companionship
jokes.punchline: Chandler's assumption that only people from happy homes want relationships
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment. Dark but funny insight into Chandler's psychology with great economy.

jokes.text: Move in with her. Move in right now. Maybe I'll move in with Monica.
jokes.setup: Gary's healthy attitude changing Chandler's perspective
jokes.punchline: Chandler immediately wanting to apply this to his own relationship
jokes.explanation: Good character growth moment. Chandler's impulsiveness when convinced.

jokes.text: No, it's too soon for you guys. You're right about that.
jokes.setup: Chandler suggesting he might move in with Monica
jokes.punchline: Gary immediately shutting down the idea, and Chandler's quick agreement
jokes.explanation: Good reversal. Gary being the practical one about Chandler's relationship.

jokes.text: Maybe this should be more of a quiet game.
jokes.setup: Ross delivering a lengthy scientific explanation during the ball game
jokes.punchline: Chandler's understated suggestion that Ross should stop talking
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan response to Ross's verbosity. Economical and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: How do you know? You don't have a watch.
jokes.setup: Monica saying she's late for work while looking for her watch
jokes.punchline: Joey's logical observation about the circular problem
jokes.explanation: Sharp logical observation from Joey. Good economy and structure.

jokes.text: Oh, I don't know... Yeah, can't do it.
jokes.setup: Monica asking them to stop playing ball to help find her watch
jokes.punchline: Joey considering it but being unable to break the streak
jokes.explanation: Shows the addictive nature of their game. Joey's brief consideration makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I won't make it in to work today. I don't feel very good. For a second, I thought I was feeling better, but I'm not.
jokes.setup: Monica calling in sick (presumably to continue the ball game)
jokes.punchline: Her fake recovery and relapse in the same phone call
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the fake illness. Monica's competitive streak overriding her usual honesty.

jokes.text: Congratulations. That's quite a waste of time.
jokes.setup: The guys proud of their three-hour ball streak
jokes.punchline: Rachel's blunt assessment of their achievement
jokes.explanation: Good reality check humor. Rachel cutting through their pride with practical observation.

jokes.text: Don't worry, it's not a cat.
jokes.setup: Monica worried about having a cat
jokes.punchline: Chandler's sarcastic reassurance using Rachel's denial
jokes.explanation: Effective callback. Short and economical callback to the running gag.

jokes.text: I dragged a string, and it flipped out and scratched the hell out of me.
jokes.setup: Rachel's expectations of cat behavior based on her grandmother's cat
jokes.punchline: The reality of her aggressive sphinx cat
jokes.explanation: Solid expectation vs reality humor. Shows the cat's true nature.

jokes.text: Every time this cat hisses at me... I know it's saying, 'Rachel!'
jokes.setup: Rachel's troubled relationship with the cat
jokes.punchline: Her paranoid belief that the cat is specifically targeting her by name
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor. The specificity of 'Rachel!' makes it funny and quotable.

jokes.text: Doesn't sound as crazy as paying $1000 for a cat.
jokes.setup: Rachel saying her belief sounds crazy
jokes.punchline: Chandler pointing out the actual crazy thing is the price
jokes.explanation: Good perspective joke. Chandler providing practical context to Rachel's paranoia.

jokes.text: You paid $1000 for a cat when you owe me $300? Well, I was gonna let you play with it.
jokes.setup: The revelation that Rachel owes Monica money
jokes.punchline: Rachel's weak justification that Monica could play with the expensive cat
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Rachel's poor financial decisions. Monica's outrage is justified.

jokes.text: You are useless! Freaking out about commitment is the one thing you can do... and you can't even do that right!
jokes.setup: Chandler failing to scare Gary away from commitment
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's frustration that Chandler can't even be properly commitment-phobic
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy. The irony of being bad at your signature flaw is brilliant.

jokes.text: No, if you're not moving forward, you're staying still. And staying still is good. Watch this.
jokes.setup: Gary's cliché about relationships moving forward or backward
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's literal demonstration of staying still being good
jokes.explanation: Great Phoebe logic. Taking the metaphor literally with physical comedy.

jokes.text: Which is why my answer is yes!
jokes.setup: Phoebe listing all the reasons not to move in together
jokes.punchline: Concluding with yes despite all the logical objections
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection. Perfect Phoebe logic - listing problems then ignoring them.

jokes.text: What? He's a dropper.
jokes.setup: Ross wanting to join the ball game
jokes.punchline: Monica's serious assessment of Ross as unreliable at ball-catching
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica competitiveness. 'Dropper' as a serious character flaw is funny.

jokes.text: Oh, yeah. That's right! I'm not a dropper!
jokes.setup: Monica calling Ross a dropper
jokes.punchline: Ross's indignant defense of his ball-catching abilities
jokes.explanation: Good escalation. Ross taking the 'dropper' label as a serious insult.

jokes.text: Don't worry, guys. It's not a cat.
jokes.setup: The cat causing chaos in the apartment
jokes.punchline: Another sarcastic use of Rachel's denial
jokes.explanation: Diminishing returns on the running gag, but still functional.

jokes.text: Baking it didn't help?
jokes.setup: Rachel giving up on the problematic cat
jokes.punchline: Chandler's deadpan suggestion about cooking the cat
jokes.explanation: Dark but funny. Excellent economy - suggests a whole failed cooking attempt in three words.

jokes.text: Maybe that's because she's a minion of the Antichrist.
jokes.setup: Discussion of why the pet store won't take the cat back
jokes.punchline: Ross's dramatic theological explanation for the cat's behavior
jokes.explanation: Good escalation. Ross bringing scholarly dramatic language to a cat problem.

jokes.text: What am I gonna do? Get 1000 regular cats?
jokes.setup: Store only offering store credit for the return
jokes.punchline: Rachel's absurd vision of exchanging one expensive cat for many cheap ones
jokes.explanation: Great visual absurdity. 1000 regular cats is a funny mental image.

jokes.text: I'm stuck with this stupid cat that looks like a hand!
jokes.setup: Rachel's frustration with the cat situation
jokes.punchline: Her description of the cat looking like a hand
jokes.explanation: Excellent visual description. 'Looks like a hand' is original and highly quotable for a hairless cat.

jokes.text: The cat's made my eyes water. Don't throw it to me! My vision's been compromised!
jokes.setup: The cat causing allergic reactions during the ball game
jokes.punchline: Chandler's dramatic description of his compromised state
jokes.explanation: 'My vision's been compromised' is overly dramatic for allergies, which makes it funny.

jokes.text: Yeah, you almost overreacted to something.
jokes.setup: Chandler saying that was close (referring to almost dropping the ball)
jokes.punchline: Joey's sarcastic observation about Chandler's tendency to overreact
jokes.explanation: Good sarcasm from Joey. Calling out Chandler's dramatic tendencies.

jokes.text: You're not more excited than I am. No way. I'm the most excited.
jokes.setup: Gary and Phoebe both claiming to be excited about moving in
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's competitive insistence that she's the most excited
jokes.explanation: Shows Phoebe's competitive side about emotions. Decent character moment.

jokes.text: Don't forget about the moving-in!
jokes.setup: Gary leaving for work after announcing they're moving in together
jokes.punchline: His awkward reminder about their big decision
jokes.explanation: Awkward but realistic. Shows Gary's earnest enthusiasm.

jokes.text: Plus think of all the money I'll save on stamps.
jokes.setup: Phoebe rationalizing why moving in together might work
jokes.punchline: Her focus on the bizarre stamp-saving benefit
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe logic. Focusing on stamps as a relationship benefit is wonderfully absurd.

jokes.text: I heard when people live together... they split the cost of stamps, don't they?
jokes.setup: Phoebe's stamp-saving comment
jokes.punchline: Her doubling down on the stamp economics despite clearly not understanding how stamps work
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the stamps absurdity. Phoebe's confusion about shared expenses.

jokes.text: Sorry, the oven mitts really freaked me out.
jokes.setup: Ross apparently having some kind of reaction during the ball game
jokes.punchline: His explanation that oven mitts scared him
jokes.explanation: Mildly funny non-sequitur. Oven mitts being scary is an odd fear.

jokes.text: If it has a pool. I need a pool.
jokes.setup: Gary saying they'll move in if an apartment opens up
jokes.punchline: His sudden specific requirement for a pool
jokes.explanation: Decent character quirk. The pool requirement feels arbitrary but realistic.

jokes.text: Oh, are these for rent? I thought people were just bragging.
jokes.setup: Phoebe confronting Gary with apartment listings from the paper
jokes.punchline: Gary's innocent misunderstanding of newspaper classified ads
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurd logic. The idea that classified ads are people bragging is wonderfully naive.

jokes.text: Yes, fine! I am someone!
jokes.setup: Gary's psychological profiling of 'someone' having doubts
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's dramatic confession to being 'someone'
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic escalation. The confession of being 'someone' is funny.

jokes.text: Let's live in an apartment we both live in.
jokes.setup: Phoebe agreeing to move in with Gary
jokes.punchline: Her circular description of living together
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe circular logic. The redundancy is endearing and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Hey, you! Behind the glass. Who are you looking at?
jokes.setup: Gary in the police station interrogation room
jokes.punchline: His cop intimidation technique on random people
jokes.explanation: Decent cop humor. Gary enjoying his interrogation room authority.

jokes.text: I always wanted to say that every time I was in these rooms, which was never.
jokes.setup: Gary's tough guy interrogation act
jokes.punchline: His admission that he's never actually been in an interrogation room
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Gary's tough guy moment. Honest and self-deprecating.

jokes.text: Then what are you doing to me?
jokes.setup: Person saying they hate cats after Rachel offers to sell it
jokes.punchline: Rachel's accusatory response about their interaction
jokes.explanation: Shows Rachel's frustration. Decent escalation of the street selling scene.

jokes.text: Oh, terrific! That'll be $2,000.
jokes.setup: Someone showing interest in adopting the cat
jokes.punchline: Rachel immediately doubling the price when she senses interest
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Rachel's desperation turning into opportunism.

jokes.text: I do, but you'll have to look at this as more of an investment than a cat.
jokes.setup: Person wanting to adopt a cat
jokes.punchline: Rachel's attempt to reframe pet ownership as financial planning
jokes.explanation: Excellent rationalization humor. Reframing a pet as an investment is absurd but convincing.

jokes.text: So $800, and I don't call the cops because you are robbing me blind! Blind! Just take the cat. Leave the money. Run away!
jokes.setup: The haggling over the cat price
jokes.punchline: Rachel's dramatic surrender while still insisting she's being robbed
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Rachel's desperation. The 'run away' instruction is funny.

jokes.text: Yeah, $1500. You made a profit?
jokes.setup: Rachel claiming she got her money back
jokes.punchline: The revelation that she actually made money on the cat
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the street selling scene. Rachel's accidental business success.

jokes.text: So is this some kind of snake or something?
jokes.setup: Gary encountering the sphinx cat for the first time
jokes.punchline: His guess that it might be a snake rather than recognizing it as a cat
jokes.explanation: Good callback to how weird the cat looks. Snake is a reasonable guess.

jokes.text: Gary's such a great guy. You can work it out. He shot a bird. That is over.
jokes.setup: Monica trying to encourage Phoebe about her relationship
jokes.punchline: The sudden revelation that Gary shot a bird, ending the relationship
jokes.explanation: Excellent sudden reversal. Perfect Phoebe dealbreaker. The economy is superb - relationship over in four words.

jokes.text: She's not an official ball player. Only official ball players can drop the ball.
jokes.setup: Phoebe apparently dropping the ball and ending their streak
jokes.punchline: Monica's desperate attempt to find a technicality to preserve their record
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica competitiveness. Creating arbitrary rules to protect their achievement.

jokes.text: How did I get this reputation as a dropper? I am anything but a dropper. Ross!
jokes.setup: Ross defending his ball-catching abilities
jokes.punchline: Presumably dropping the ball immediately after his defense
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic timing. Ross proving he's a dropper by immediately dropping. Classic physical comedy setup.

tmdb_overview: Phoebe worries about Gary's move-in proposal; Ross and Joey's competitive ball game escalates when Monica joins.
